key_points: More from TechCrunch ChatGPT’s market share slips below 50% for first time More than three and a half years after ChatGPT’s initial release, AI assistants are now used by millions of people worldwide, and the.
The chatbot still remains the most popular AI assistant worldwide with over 1.1 billion monthly users, followed by Gemini with 662 million and Claude with 245 million.

While OpenAI’s chatbot is still the most popular assistant globally, its market share has dipped below 50% for the first time as users are migrating between different assistants like Google’s Gemini, Anthropic’s Claude.
In the first half of 2026, people are on pace to download nearly 2.3 billion AI apps and spend over $4.2 billion on them, according to Sensor Tower estimates.
Regionally, Asia recorded the first download decline of 3.3% in Q1 2026, driven by dips in China and India.
Software and shopping are the largest advertiser categories in ChatGPT so far, followed by media and entertainment and food and dining.

SpaceX to acquire Cursor for $60B in stock, days after blockbuster IPO Amazon CEO reportedly raised Anthropic model concerns before government crackdown The FBI built its own replica small town to simulate real-world.
Until January, ChatGPT commanded over 50% market share, but by May’s end, it had fallen to 46.4% thanks to the rise of Gemini (27.7%) and Claude (10.3%).
The top three assistants command 89% time spent on AI assistant apps.
Sensor Tower estimates that the hours spent on AI apps will have increased from 17.2 billion hours in H1 2025 to roughly 36 billion hours in H1 2026.
It became the fastest app ever to reach 1 billion monthly users, as Sensor Tower reported this month.
Notably, OpenAI counts weekly active users, and it last reported 900 million of them in February .

article_items.title: Probably raises $9M to build a more reliable kind of AI | TechCrunch
article_items.description: Probably wants to prevent hallucinations and factual errors from reaching users, and achieve accuracy on par with deterministic systems.
article_items.excerpt: Probably wants to prevent hallucinations and factual errors from reaching users, and achieve accuracy on par with deterministic systems.
article_items.summary: Basically, it’s an exercise in reducing ambiguity.” That allows Probably’s data science tool to run on significantly smaller AI models. Probably , which just raised $9 million in seed funding from Andreessen Horowitz, is trying to build a more rigorous way to catch those errors. Crucially, the LLM has been trained against the validator, and the whole system is optimized for fast and accurate answers, the company said. As founder Peter Elias (pictured above) puts it, the company’s goal is to prevent hallucinations and simple factual errors from ever reaching the user, and achieve the kind of 99.99% accuracy that’s common in deterministic systems but much more difficult to reach with AI. Elias says the current version is running on a model that’s “four classes weaker than the frontier models,” which means it can be run on local hardware (that is, a desktop computer instead of a data center), which reduces a huge amount of the token costs associated with AI use.

article_items.title: The Art of Loop Engineering
article_items.description: Agents automate real-world work, but reliable performance requires more than a good model, it requires a carefully designed harness built for specific tasks. This post explores the core agent loop, how stacking and extending loops builds more effective agents, and how to instrument each level with LangChain primitives.
article_items.excerpt: Agents automate real-world work, but reliable performance requires more than a good model, it requires a carefully designed harness built for specific tasks. This post explores the core agent loop, how stacking and extending loops builds more effective agents, and how to instrument each level with LangChain primitives.

key_points: The key move here is that the return arrow doesn't just loop back to the top - it reaches inside and updates the agent loop directly.
Level 3: Event driven loop One of the most important parts of agent development is the integrations layer: connecting your agent to your ecosystem so that it can run in the background.
This is the most fundamental loop.
Level 2: Verification loop The agent loop gets work done, but it doesn't always produce correct or consistent work on the first pass.
S e e w h a t y o u r a g e n t i s r e a l l y d o i n g LangSmith, our agent engineering platform, helps developers debug every agent decision, eval changes, and deploy in one click.
Wrapping up the docs agent analogy, we run engine over the docs agent traces to detect any issues.
The loop is the pattern; what it optimizes is up to you.
The core agent algorithm is simple: give the LLM context and let it call tools in a loop until it's done.
Tools are what give the agent the power to take action in the real world.
Every agent run produce a trace: a record of what the model did, the tools it called, grader feedback, etc.
The event-driven loop connects your agent to your ecosystem.
The hill climbing loop runs an analysis agent over those traces and uses the findings to rewrite the harness with improved configuration.

article_items.title: Introducing Genie One, Genie Agents, and Genie Ontology
article_items.description: Genie One is the next evolution of Genie, designed for business users. It leverages Genie Ontology to connect to all your systems, and gives everyone a data-smart AI coworker to automate their work.
article_items.excerpt: Genie One is the next evolution of Genie, designed for business users. It leverages Genie Ontology to connect to all your systems, and gives everyone a data-smart AI coworker to automate their work.

key_points: Partner Program Explore benefits, tiers and how to become a partner Partner Solutions Find custom industry and migration solutions Platform Overview A unified platform for data, analytics and AI Application Development.

Genie One: a data-smart AI coworker that works across all of your data, Genie Agents: a way for everyone to create dedicated agents to automate work, and Genie Ontology: an automatic and secure context store that.
Genie delivers high accuracy and low latency, 2× faster than the strongest coding agent.
In our testing, Genie answered 84.5% of questions correctly on the first attempt, while the strongest general-purpose coding agent managed just 52.4% - and the weakest only 25% .
Genie Agents let teams turn prompts into shareable, autonomous agents that can reason over structured and unstructured data, and take action across business tools.
Our internal benchmark of real-world enterprise data analysis tasks have shown that Genie Ontology significantly improves agent performance on complex, enterprise data questions.
For all of your apps and data.
is hard even with the latest generations of models and agents.

Best of all, creating an agent is as simple as describing what you want: spin up a Genie Agent from a single prompt in Genie One or Genie Code, scope it, benchmark it, and share it for teammates to use or customize.

And for organizations who have adopted an existing AI agent or developed their own, we’re also announcing the Genie MCP App, which allows those users to benefit from Genie without having to change their workflows.
Genie Ontology: a living context graph for your business Genie One and Genie Agents are powered by Genie Ontology , an automatic context layer.

rag_rules: Make the business move concrete: who is involved, what changed, and why now.
Tie funding, partnerships, or revenue to product direction or competitive pressure.
Avoid hype words unless the article gives evidence.
Lead with the actual product, model, or API name.
